title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
friendlyTitle: Researchers found a new way to look inside Claude
summary: MIT Technology Review covered Anthropic’s new Jacobian lens research, which probes a hidden internal area the researchers call J-space inside Claude Opus 4.6. The work aims to reveal concepts the model is processing before it produces visible output, with a public demo made through Neuronpedia.
friendlySummary: Anthropic researchers built a tool that can show some of the ideas Claude may be processing before it answers.
whyItMatters: As AI agents take on more consequential work, interpretability tools become part of the trust stack. Better visibility into model internals could help researchers debug failures, detect risky behavior, and design safer systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could help people understand and improve AI systems, especially when they make mistakes.
